item.title: Business Owners Everywhere Can’t Escape Iran War’s Ripple Effects
item.description: From lost income to worsening inflation, small and midsize operators around the globe are feeling the pain.

item.title: Two Indian Conglomerates to Plow $1 Billion in EV, Battery Tech
item.description: Two Indian conglomerates are looking to spend nearly $1 billion to build domestic capabilities in electric-vehicle and battery technologies, people familiar with the matter said, reflecting a growing urgency on the part of the country’s biggest companies to reduce their reliance on Chinese technology.

item.title: UK Construction Slump Gets Worse as Cost Pressures Intensify
item.description: British builders posted the largest decline in output since November as the Iran war pushed up input prices and discouraged sales.

item.title: US, Iran Deliberate Peace Deal With Nuclear Breakthrough Distant
item.description: The US is waiting on Iran to respond to its proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end a war that’s killed thousands of people and triggered a surge in energy prices.

item.title: Saudi Wealth Fund to Sell First Dollar Bonds Since Iran War
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund is selling a multi-tranche US dollar bond, a sign Gulf issuers are returning to public markets after disruption tied to the Iran conflict.

item.title: Amazon Cuts Workers, Phases Out Grocery Service in Singapore
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. said a “small number of roles” in Singapore will be impacted as it phases out local fulfillment including Amazon Fresh in the city-state.

item.title: French Container Ship Makes Rare Crossing of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A French containership has emerged in the Arabian Sea after last being seen in the Persian Gulf, indicating a rare crossing of the Strait of Hormuz by a Western European vessel.

item.title: Norway Hikes for First Time Since 2023, Taking Lead in Europe
item.description: Norges Bank delivered western Europe’s first hike in borrowing costs since the outbreak of the Iran war, opting for immediate action to tackle stubbornly high inflation.

item.title: How an American City Can Become a Manufacturing Hub
item.description: A conversation with the mayor of Allentown, Pennsylvania.

item.title: Odd Lots: How a US City Can Become a Manufacturing Hub (Podcast)
item.description: The residents of Allentown are still sore about that Billy Joel song. While it’s true the Pennsylvania city became synonymous with deindustrialization after the US steel industry began its decline in the 1970s, Allentown should be known for more: In the 1950s, for instance, some of the first mass-produced transistors were made in the city, which were the precursor of today’s semiconductors. The city is also a unique logistics and e-commerce hub — it’s a day’s drive from nearly 40 percent of the

item.title: UBS Fined €6 Million by Monaco Over Repeated Dirty Money Failures
item.description: UBS Group AG was fined €6 million ($7 million) in Monaco over weak anti-money laundering controls, including filing a report 253 days after a suspicious transaction.

item.title: Jet Fuel Concerns Hang Over Europe as Summer Vacations Approach
item.description: In a matter of weeks, Europeans will begin heading off on their summer vacations — whether or not there will be enough jet fuel to transport them all remains an open question.

item.title: Saudi Airline Faces Difficult Launch as Iran War Upends Travel
item.description: When Saudi Arabia unveiled plans in 2023 for a new airline that could take on regional champions Emirates and Qatar Airways, the aviation industry was back in full swing after the pandemic, with packed planes driving fares higher and lifting profits to a record.

item.title: Earnings Beats in Europe Mask Tougher Times Ahead for Stocks
item.description: A strong earnings season is hiding tougher times ahead for European stocks as the effects of the Iran war make it harder for companies to meet lofty profit expectations in the quarters to come.

item.title: Chipmaker Silex Microsystems Shares Soar in Stockholm Debut
item.description: Silex Microsystems AB surged in its first day of trading on Nasdaq Stockholm after an initial public offering that raised about 2 billion Swedish kronor ($220 million) for the specialist microchip-maker and its Chinese shareholder Sai MicroElectronics Inc.

item.title: Aliko Dangote’s Group Eyes More Dollar Bond Sales to Fund Growth
item.description: Dangote Industries Ltd is seeking more opportunities to raise money from global investors after the African company made its first foray into international markets last month.

item.title: Malaysia Holds Rate as Ringgit Tempers Inflation Pressure
item.description: Malaysia held its benchmark interest rate steady for a fifth straight meeting, despite heightened risks to growth from the prolonged conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: EU Startup Fund Makes First Bet With $160 Million Round in UK’s Quantum Motion
item.description: Kembara, a technology growth fund backed by the European Union, made its first investment, supporting a British startup that builds quantum computers running on silicon chips.

item.title: Brookfield Bets on Dubai Real Estate, Defying War Concerns
item.description: Private equity giant Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. is setting up a new property joint venture in Dubai with Gulf consumer giant Alshaya Group, a bold bet on the city’s real estate market even as the Iran war enters its third month.

item.title: Maersk CEO Sees Passing Higher Oil Shock Costs to Customers
item.description: The chief executive officer of A.P. Moller-Maersk A/S said the oil shock caused by the Iran war will significantly raise costs this quarter and next, which the world’s No. 2 container carrier will seek to fully pass on to customers.

item.title: Shell Profit Rises as Iran War Boosts Oil Price and Trading
item.description: Shell Plc said its profit surged in the first quarter as the Iran war drove oil and gas prices higher and the conflict caused a increase in volatility that boosted its big trading business.

item.title: AirAsia to Start New Airline, Stays Defiant on Fuel Hedging
item.description: AirAsia X Bhd. co-founder Tony Fernandes said he’s preparing to launch a new airline, betting that expanding while the aviation industry’s being roiled by high oil prices will pay off in the future.

item.title: Private Markets Could Support Europe’s Financing Push, ECB Says
item.description: The European Central Bank said burgeoning private markets could support a push by politicians in the region to channel savings into investments needed to modernize the economy.

item.title: FTSE 100 Poised to Rise on Iran Deal Optimism
Asian Bond Issuers Rush to Market as Iran Hopes Tighten Spreads
item.description: Asia’s dollar bond market has seen a burst of issuance as companies and governments rushed to secure cheaper funding, with credit spreads tightening to record lows on hopes tensions in the Middle East may ease.

item.title: Metals Rally Stalls as Traders Await Iran Reply to US Proposal
item.description: Copper edged lower with aluminum while Iran evaluates a fresh proposal from US President Donald Trump to end a conflict that’s nearing the 10-week mark.

item.title: Vodacom Kenya Unit Posts Record Profit, Cuts Ethiopia Losses
item.description: East African telecommunications operator Safaricom Plc’s annual profit jumped 67%, beating analysts’ expectations, after its unit in populous Ethiopia narrowed its loss.

item.title: Intrum to Raise $812 Million of Equity Capital to Trim Debt
item.description: Swedish credit management firm Intrum AB plans to raise 7.5 billion Swedish kronor ($812 million) of equity capital, as the company continues to struggle with a heavy debt load.

item.title: Europe’s Worst Earnings Are Coming From Luxury and Auto Sectors
item.description: Europe’s luxury firms, carmakers and hotels largely disappointed investors this earnings season, making consumer discretionary the worst-performing sector as rising inflation and geopolitical uncertainty derail a long-awaited recovery.

item.title: Inflation Fears Are Making Linkers a Winning Bond Trade in 2026
item.description: The last time oil prices spiked due to war in 2022, the rush to get protection with inflation-linked bonds turned out badly. So far, this time is proving different.
